name: Chernobyl - April 26, 1986
description: RBMK-1000 Unit 4 disaster. During a turbine rundown test, operators reduced power but it unexpectedly collapsed to 30 MWt. To recover, they withdrew nearly all control rods against void collapse, graphite cooling, and rising xenon — ORM fell to 6-8 rods (minimum 15 required). When AZ-5 was pressed, graphite displacer tips on the control rods added positive reactivity. The resulting power excursion deformed fuel channels, jamming rods at ~2m of 7m travel. Prompt supercritical excursion destroyed the reactor.

events.message: 25 APR 23:10 — Power reduction resumes from 1,600 MWt (50%)
Grid controller delayed test 9 hours. Xe-135 in equilibrium at 1,600 MWt.
26 APR 00:28 — Control transfer. Auto regulators OFF.
Setpoint error — rods driving deep, power dropping...
Power suppressed. Void collapse + graphite cooling absorb reactivity.
Rods deep in core. Multiple reactivity losses demand withdrawal.
Operators withdraw rods — void collapse, cooling graphite, rising Xe...
01:00 — Power low but unstable. ORM already violated.
Manual rod control. Operators fighting reactivity losses.
Withdrawing rods to maintain power against multiple reactivity losses...
VIOLATION: Rods withdrawn past operating limits!
01:19 — Extra feedwater pumps ON. Coolant flow increased.
Increased coolant flow suppresses boiling temporarily
01:22:30 — ORM at 6–8 rods. Minimum required: 15. (INSAG-7)
01:23:04 — TURBINE TEST BEGINS. Feed valves closed.
Turbine coasting down. Coolant pumps losing power...
Coolant flow dropping! Steam forming in pressure channels.
Coolant flow dropping. Void fraction rising in pressure channels.
01:23:30 — Void coefficient feedback accelerating. Power well above nominal.
01:23:40 — AZ-5 pressed. Routine or precautionary shutdown.
AZ-5 PRESSED — Graphite displacer tips enter core first!
POSITIVE SCRAM: Tips displace water → reactivity spike!
01:23:43 — Power surge continues. Fuel under extreme stress!
Rods at ~2–2.5m of 7m travel. Reactor destruction imminent!
01:23:47 — PROMPT SUPERCRITICAL — Reactor beyond any recovery.
Reactor 4 destroyed. Two explosions. 26 April 1986, 01:23:47.
